command:
cargo run --release --bin resinsim -- report health --file data/test_cube_60mm.stl --n-supports 0 --json 2>/dev/null | jq '{
  layers: .summary.total_layers,
  max_force_n: (.summary.max_peel_force_n | . * 100 | round / 100),
  min_sf: (.summary.min_safety_factor | . * 100 | round / 100),
  max_z_um: (.summary.max_z_deflection_um | . * 10 | round / 10),
  critical: .summary.critical_failures,
  warnings: .summary.warnings,
  failure_types: [.failures[].type] | group_by(.) | map({type: .[0], count: length}),
  severity: [.failures[].severity] | group_by(.) | map({s: .[0], count: length})
}'

echo "=== Budget printer k=460, 50µm layers ===" && cargo run --release --bin resinsim -- inspect zaxis --force 46.8 --stiffness 460 --layer-height 50 2>/dev/null && echo "" && echo "=== Budget printer k=460, 100µm layers ===" && cargo run --release --bin resinsim -- inspect zaxis --force 46.8 --stiffness 460 --layer-height 100 2>/dev/null && echo "" && echo "=== Mars 5 Ultra k=1000, 50µm ===" && cargo run --release --bin resinsim -- inspect zaxis --force 46.8 --stiffness 1000 --layer-height 50 2>/dev/null && echo "" && echo "=== Athena II k=1500, 50µm ===" && cargo run --release --bin resinsim -- inspect zaxis --force 46.8 --stiffness 1500 --layer-height 50 2>/dev/null

done

jj commit -m "T1-F1: Remove duplicated degradation/cold methods from VatTemperature

Remove is_degradation_risk and is_too_cold from VatTemperature (values layer).
These methods imported ResinProfile (entity), creating a values→entities circular
dependency. ResinProfile already carries canonical versions of both methods.

- Delete both methods from VatTemperature in thermal.rs
- Delete orphaned tests degradation_above_50c, too_cold_below_15c from thermal.rs
  (coverage already exists in resin_profile.rs:212-224)
- Remove unused 'use crate::entities::ResinProfile' from thermal.rs test module
- Migrate thermal_calculator.rs:212 to call resin.is_degradation_risk(t)

324/324 tests passing." 2>&1
swamp model method run t1f1 review_code 2>&1 | tail -3
swamp --repo-dir /Users/mag1/dev_tmp/ora model method run t1f1 review_code 2>&1 | tail -3

cd /Users/mag1/dev_tmp/ora/resinsim && jj commit -m "T1-F1 harvest: ADR-0001, DDD anti-pattern, UAT thermal-degradation

- docs/adr/0001-ddd-layer-dependency-rule.md: values must not import entities
- docs/patterns/anti/entity-predicate-on-value.md: anti-pattern with before/after code
- spec/uat/thermal-degradation.md: end-to-end thermal degradation detection scenario" 2>&1

cd /Users/mag1/dev_tmp/ora/resinsim && cargo nextest run -p resinsim-core 2>&1 | tail -6

jj describe --message "T1-F2: SafetyFactor::compute returns Option<SafetyFactor>

Remove f32::INFINITY sentinel. None explicitly models no-load (zero peel force).
SafetyFactor invariants now self-consistent: new() rejects INFINITY, compute()
never produces it.

- compute() returns None when force <= 0, Some(ratio) otherwise
- Fix misleading doc comment on new()
- Rename test: safety_factor_infinity_for_zero_force -> safety_factor_none_for_zero_force
- failure_predictor: map_or(false,...) guard + unwrap() inside block
- failure_predictor LayerResult: map_or(f32::INFINITY,...) at output boundary
- resinsim-inspect: (Some(cap), sf) — drop redundant Some() wrapper
- force_properties: .unwrap() on non-zero-force prop test calls

324/324 tests passing." 2>&1

cd /Users/mag1/dev_tmp/ora/resinsim && jj new -m "T1-F2 harvest: ADR-0002, split-constructor anti-pattern, UAT zero-force

- docs/adr/0002-option-not-sentinel-for-absent-values.md
- docs/patterns/anti/split-constructor-invariant.md
- spec/uat/safety-factor-zero-force.md" 2>&1

python3 -c "
import re
with open('crates/resinsim-core/src/services/failure_predictor.rs', 'r') as f:
    content = f.read()
# Only replace in the #[cfg(test)] section
test_start = content.index('#[cfg(test)]')
prod = content[:test_start]
test = content[test_start:]
test = re.sub(r'CrossSectionArea\((\d+(?:\.\d+)?)\)', r'CrossSectionArea::new(\1).unwrap()', test)
with open('crates/resinsim-core/src/services/failure_predictor.rs', 'w') as f:
    f.write(prod + test)
print('done')
" 2>&1

with open('crates/resinsim-core/src/services/thermal_calculator.rs', 'w') as f:

jj commit -m "T1-F3: Close value-object invariant bypasses — enforce constructor invariants end-to-end

Steps 1-12 of plan v4:
- Energy::scale (infallible, debug_assert) + from_exposure → Result
- CrossSectionArea::circle → Result  
- Remove pub(crate) from all 10 value struct fields (compile-error on tuple bypass)
- Fix all production bypass sites: 3-tier strategy
  - ? propagation: simulation_runner CTB user data
  - .expect() with profile validate() citation: failure_predictor (7 sites),
    peel_force_calculator (4), thermal_calculator, cure_calculator, suction_detector
  - NaN-safe clamp: geometry.rs shoelace area
- Profile validate() enforced at run_from_areas + run_from_layer_inputs entry points
  (closes release-build gap where debug_assert on scale() would be silently dropped)
- Test fixture fixes in 10 service/entity files
- Integration test callers updated (kb_golden_values, kb_validation)
- Regression tests: from_exposure 5 cases, circle 4 cases, profile fixture invariant
  assertions, invalid-profile-returns-Err entry point test" 2>&1 | tail -5

jj commit -m "T1-F4: add ec>0 guard and close NaN gaps in Energy and cure_depth

- Energy::new rejects NaN and infinite values (NaN <= 0.0 is false in Rust,
  so NaN previously slipped through the > 0 check)
- Energy::scale upgraded from debug_assert! to assert! so the NaN-factor
  guard fires in release builds as well as debug
- CureCalculator::cure_depth gains runtime assert guards for both energy and
  critical_energy, with clear panic messages — last-line defence covering
  any bypass path (e.g. scale with NaN factor in production via
  uniformity_calculator::cure_depth_at_position)
- Three regression tests: Energy::new(NaN)->Err, Energy::new(Inf)->Err,
  Energy::scale(NaN) via #[should_panic], transmute-bypass guard test

Closes: T1-F4  Follow-up: PenetrationDepth::new has same NaN gap (out of scope)"

git -C /Users/mag1/dev_tmp/ora/resinsim commit \
  -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
T1-F4: close NaN gaps in Energy, PenetrationDepth, and CureCalculator

Root causes:
- Energy::new and PenetrationDepth::new validated > 0 but NaN passes
  <= 0.0 in Rust (NaN comparisons always return false)
- Energy::scale bypassed Energy::new with only a debug_assert!, so
  a NaN factor slipped through in release builds

Fixes:
- Energy::new: add !mj_cm2.is_finite() check
- PenetrationDepth::new: add !um.is_finite() check
- Energy::scale: debug_assert! → assert! (closes release-mode bypass
  via uniformity_calculator::cure_depth_at_position)
- CureCalculator::cure_depth: runtime assert guards for energy and
  critical_energy with clear panic messages

Tests (6 new):
- Energy::new(NaN) → Err
- Energy::new(Inf) → Err
- Energy::scale(NaN) → #[should_panic]
- PenetrationDepth::new(NaN) → Err
- PenetrationDepth::new(Inf) → Err
- cure_depth transmute-bypass guard test

Closes: T1-F4
EOF
)" crates/resinsim-core/src/services/cure_calculator.rs crates/resinsim-core/src/values/cure_depth.rs
